title: The API that doesn't exist
summary: The AI called a tidy-looking method. Does it actually exist?
task: Trim whitespace from both ends of every string in an array.

hiddenIssues.title: String.prototype.trimBoth is hallucinated
hiddenIssues.explanation: There is no `trimBoth()` on strings — the AI invented a plausible-sounding name. `tsc` flags it instantly: "Property 'trimBoth' does not exist on type 'string'". The real method is `.trim()`.

debrief: Hallucinated APIs are one of the most common AI failures — they look right because the *name* is plausible. A type check catches them instantly, which is exactly why 'did you even run tsc?' matters. (A build would catch it too, since it type-checks; a linter usually won't.)
